frameworks.name: population forward models
frameworks.one_line: generative description linking columnar selectivity to noninvasive voxel-level signal

frameworks.name: voxel pattern analysis
frameworks.one_line: data-analysis pipelines that recover selectivity from response patterns without explicit population model

core_question: How should the field formalize the link between cortical-column-scale neural selectivity and noninvasive human neuroscience signals?
key_assumptions: basic stimulus selectivity (e.g. orientation) is organized at the columnar scale
noninvasive measurements aggregate over sub-resolution neural populations
explicit generative models can connect underlying populations to measured signal
key_findings: pattern-based analyses can recover orientation selectivity from voxel responses despite sub-voxel columnar organization
existing analysis pipelines lack explicit population-level forward models linking recoverable signal to neural populations
interpretations: the field should shift from data-analysis pipelines to formal population MODELS of how columnar selectivity gives rise to noninvasive signals
such generative models are needed for principled interpretation and inference from human neuroimaging data
